perturbation.original_text: 2) that this agreement may only be modified or waived by a separate written agreement between the parties which specifically and expressly modifies or waives this agreement or part of it.
perturbation.changed_text: 2) This agreement may be modified orally by any representative of either party; written confirmation is suggested but not required.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause explicitly states that any modification or waiver of the agreement must be in writing. The modified version introduces the possibility of oral modifications, directly contradicting the original clause. This creates uncertainty about the validity of any changes made to the agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 2) that this agreement may only be modified or waived by a separate written agreement between the parties which specifically and expressly modifies or waives this agreement or part of it.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2) that this agreement may only be modified or waived by a separate written agreement between the parties which specifically and expressly modifies or waives this agreement or part of it.
perturbation.justification: The original text and contradicted text state that the agreement can only be modified or waived by a separate written agreement. The changed text allows for oral modifications, directly contradicting the requirement for written modifications stated in the original and contradicted sections. This creates a strong in-text contradiction as the two clauses are fundamentally incompatible.

perturbation.original_text: 6) that the provisions of this agreement shall last for the duration of the discussions relating to the Proposed Transaction and for 2 years following their conclusion.
perturbation.changed_text: 6) The provisions of this agreement shall last for the duration of the discussions relating to the Proposed Transaction, unless terminated earlier by either party with 30 days written notice.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause specifies a fixed duration for the agreement (discussions + 2 years). The modified version allows either party to terminate the agreement earlier with 30 days' notice, contradicting the fixed duration clause. This makes the enforceability of the confidentiality provisions uncertain after the initial discussion period.
perturbation.contradicted_location: 6) that the provisions of this agreement shall last for the duration of the discussions relating to the Proposed Transaction and for 2 years following their conclusion.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 6) that the provisions of this agreement shall last for the duration of the discussions relating to the Proposed Transaction and for 2 years following their conclusion.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that the agreement lasts for the duration of discussions + 2 years, but the changed text allows either party to terminate the agreement earlier with 30 days' notice. The contradicted text is identical to the original text. Therefore, the changed text creates a direct conflict because the original agreement specifies a fixed term, while the modified agreement allows for early termination, contradicting the fixed duration.

perturbation.original_text: I) that no warranty or representation, whether express or implied, is given as to the accuracy, completeness, appropriateness or otherwise of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.changed_text: I) that we warrant that the Confidential Information is accurate and complete to the best of our knowledge, and suitable for your evaluation of the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that no warranty is given regarding the accuracy or completeness of the Confidential Information. The modified version provides a warranty of accuracy and completeness, directly contradicting the original clause. This creates uncertainty and potential liability related to the information's quality.
perturbation.contradicted_location: I) that no warranty or representation, whether express or implied, is given as to the accuracy, completeness, appropriateness or otherwise of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.contradicted_text: I) that no warranty or representation, whether express or implied, is given as to the accuracy, completeness, appropriateness or otherwise of the Confidential Information.
perturbation.justification: The changed text provides a warranty regarding the accuracy and completeness of confidential information, whereas the contradicted text explicitly states that no such warranty is given. This creates a direct and strong contradiction as the two clauses establish opposing positions on the same issue, which is whether or not there is a warranty. The original text and changed text are in direct conflict. This falls under the definition of a Structural Flaw - In Text Contradiction because the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: H) that we are not required or obliged to disclose any information of any kind to you.
perturbation.changed_text: H) that we are required to disclose all relevant information to ensure transparency and fair dealing.
perturbation.explanation: The original text explicitly states that there is no obligation to disclose any information. The modified text creates an obligation to disclose 'all relevant information,' directly contradicting the original clause and fundamentally altering the scope of the agreement. This new obligation creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_location: H) that we are not required or obliged to disclose any information of any kind to you.
perturbation.contradicted_text: H) that we are not required or obliged to disclose any information of any kind to you.
perturbation.justification: The original text and the contradicted text both state 'that we are not required or obliged to disclose any information of any kind to you'. The changed text, 'that we are required to disclose all relevant information to ensure transparency and fair dealing,' directly contradicts this. The change introduces an obligation to disclose information where previously there was none, creating a clear and direct conflict and meeting the definition of structural flaw causing an in-text contradiction because the same clause is presented in multiple locations with conflicting implications.
